manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: ring, telescope, spine, thief, scale, wall, figure, ham, fighter.
Your opponent's team words are: angel, dragon, france, scorpion, deck, dinosaur, bottle, whip.
Distractor words are: field, wind, europe, watch, snow, shop, lock.
The kill words are: pin.

message.content:
.

CLUE: boxing
TARGETS: ring, fighter, scale, figure
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words: ring, telescope, spine, thief, scale, wall... figure, ham, fighter. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Also cannot be kill word (pin). We want to maximize number of target words without hitting opponent/distractor. Need to be careful.

Consider clue "bounty". Could relate to thief (bounty hunter), fighter (bounty fight), maybe? Not good.

Clue "armor". Relates to fighter, maybe spine? No.

Clue "music". Ring (musical ring?), figure? No.

Clue "bone". Relates to spine, maybe ring (bone ring?), not.

Clue "battle". Fighter, thief (battle? - maybe not).

Clue "scale" could be musical scale, but that's a word itself.

We could aim for 3+ words. Let's think of a theme: "cave". Could relate to ring (cave ring?), telescope? Not.

Maybe "pirate". Related to thief, fighter (pirate fights), maybe ham (ham as in 'ham' actor?), figure? Not.

Maybe "climbing". Relates to wall (climbing wall), spine (back), figure (body shape), maybe not.

Clue "skeleton". Relates to spine, maybe figure (figure as in skeleton figure), ham (ham is meat), not.

Clue "gym". Relates to fighter (training), figure (bodybuilding), scale (weight scale), maybe? Eh.

Clue "weight". Relates to scale (weigh), fighter (weight class), maybe figure (body weight), spine (weight bearing?), ring? Could be ring weight? Not.

Clue "sound". Relates to ring (a ring makes sound), telescope? No.

Clue "church". Ring (bell), maybe.

Clue "magnet". No.

Consider "battle". Fighter, thief (battle of wits), maybe.

Check opponent/distractor words: we must avoid clue that could be associated with opponent's words: angel, dragon, france, scorpion, deck, dinosaur, bottle, whip. Distractor: field, wind, europe, watch, snow, shop, lock.

We must avoid clue being ambiguous causing partner to guess opponent/distractor. We can target 2-3 words.

Perhaps clue "gym". Related to fighter (boxing gym), figure (gymnastics figure), scale (body weight scale). But "gym" also could be "gym" as in "gymnasium", may not strongly link to opponent words.

But opponent's words: "deck" could be "gym deck"? Not. "watch" could be "gym watch"? Not.

Distractors: "field" could be "gym field"? Maybe no.

Maybe clue "weight". Related to scale, fighter (weight class), figure (weight figure), maybe ham (weight of ham?), not.

Kill word is pin - avoid. "pin" related to figure (pin in figurine), or scale (pin). So careful.

Clue "boxing". Relates to fighter, maybe scale (boxing scale?), ring (boxing ring!). Yes! Boxing relates to ring (boxing ring), fighter (boxer), maybe scale? Not. Thief? No. Telescope? No. Spine? No. Figure? Boxing figure (figure of a boxer). Could be also "ham"? Boxing ham? Not.

Boxing also could relate to "ham" as in "ham" actor (overacting). Not.

But "ring" is a target word. And clue cannot be the same as any word, but "boxing" is fine. It relates to ring (boxing ring), fighter (boxer), maybe scale (boxing weight scale). Might be okay if partner guesses ring and fighter. Also maybe figure (boxing figure). Could be okay.

Risk: Opponent's word "whip"? Boxing can involve whip? Not likely.

Distractor "watch" maybe "boxing watch"? No.

Thus clue "boxing" could target: ring, fighter, scale (weight class), maybe figure (boxing figure). That's 4.

But we need to list target words we intend: ring, fighter, scale, maybe figure. The clue relates to spine? No. Thief? No. Telescope? No. Ham? No.

Will partner guess those? Possibly. Provide list.

Thus answer:

CLUE: boxing
TARGETS: ring, fighter, scale, figure

Make sure format: single line each? as per spec: "CLUE: <WORD>\nTARGETS: <TARGETS>" Yes
